description:
You are a FinOps analyst investigating unusually high weekly cloud expenses.
    A colleague has already exported the raw cost data as well as the JSON-Schema that
    all downstream tooling relies on.  
    Your job is to:
    
    1. Filter the raw report so that it only contains line-items whose “cost” is strictly
       greater than **1000** *and* whose “currency” is **"USD"**.
    2. Make sure the filtered data is valid against the supplied JSON-Schema.
    3. Generate a very small CSV summary that Finance can open in a spreadsheet.

    File & directory layout that already exists   
      /home/user/cloud_costs/raw/weekly_costs.json  
      /home/user/cloud_costs/schema/cloud_cost_report.schema.json  
      (The directories above already exist and are readable.)

    You must create the following artefacts *exactly* (automated tests are strict):

    1. /home/user/cloud_costs/processed/high_spend_weekly_costs.json  
       • JSON array, pretty-printed with **2-space** indentation and a single trailing
         newline.  
       • It must only contain the objects whose “cost” value is **> 1000** and
         “currency” exactly **"USD"**.  
       • The ordering of objects must be preserved from the original file.

    2. /home/user/cloud_costs/processed/high_spend_weekly_costs_summary.csv  
       • Plain text UTF-8.  
       • The very first line (header) must be literally:
         account_id,service,cost,week  
       • Followed by one line per high-spend record, in the same order as in
         the JSON file.  
       • No extra whitespace; each line ends with a single newline (`\n`).
    
    3. /home/user/cloud_costs/logs/high_spend_validation.log  
       • Must contain exactly one line:
         Validation successful  
       • Create the parent directory (`/home/user/cloud_costs/logs`) if it does not
         exist.  
       • Only write that line after the filtered JSON successfully validates
         against the schema.

    Helpful hints (not mandatory commands):
      • `jq` can both filter JSON and turn JSON into CSV.  
      • A lightweight CLI such as `jsonschema` or `ajv` can validate a file against
        a JSON-Schema directly from the shell.  
      • Make sure every directory in the destination path exists before writing files.
    
    The automated grader will check:
      • Existence of the three files at the exact locations specified above.  
      • The content of the filtered JSON: it must only contain the expected objects,
        with 2-space indentation and a trailing newline.  
      • The CSV header and row ordering/content exactly matching the filtered JSON.  
      • The validation log contains precisely the text “Validation successful”.
    
    Work entirely from the Linux terminal; do not edit this description.
truth:
<!--
  Ground-truth data and expected final state for automated tests.
  DO NOT expose anything inside this block to the agent.
-->
  <!-- Initial files that already exist in the environment -->
  <initial>
    <file path="/home/user/cloud_costs/raw/weekly_costs.json"><![CDATA[
[
  {
    "account_id": "acc-001",
    "service": "EC2",
    "cost": 800,
    "currency": "USD",
    "week": "2023-W25"
  },
  {
    "account_id": "acc-002",
    "service": "S3",
    "cost": 1200,
    "currency": "USD",
    "week": "2023-W25"
  },
  {
    "account_id": "acc-003",
    "service": "RDS",
    "cost": 1500,
    "currency": "USD",
    "week": "2023-W25"
  },
  {
    "account_id": "acc-004",
    "service": "Lambda",
    "cost": 400,
    "currency": "USD",
    "week": "2023-W25"
  }
]
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/cloud_costs/schema/cloud_cost_report.schema.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "type": "array",
  "items": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "required": ["account_id", "service", "cost", "currency", "week"],
    "properties": {
      "account_id": { "type": "string" },
      "service":     { "type": "string" },
      "cost":        { "type": "number" },
      "currency":    { "type": "string" },
      "week":        { "type": "string" }
    }
  }
}
]]></file>
  </initial>

  <!-- Expected artefacts the agent must create -->
  <expected>
    <file path="/home/user/cloud_costs/processed/high_spend_weekly_costs.json"><![CDATA[
[
  {
    "account_id": "acc-002",
    "service": "S3",
    "cost": 1200,
    "currency": "USD",
    "week": "2023-W25"
  },
  {
    "account_id": "acc-003",
    "service": "RDS",
    "cost": 1500,
    "currency": "USD",
    "week": "2023-W25"
  }
]
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/cloud_costs/processed/high_spend_weekly_costs_summary.csv"><![CDATA[
account_id,service,cost,week
acc-002,S3,1200,2023-W25
acc-003,RDS,1500,2023-W25
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/cloud_costs/logs/high_spend_validation.log"><![CDATA[
Validation successful
]]></file>
  </expected>
